mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4% triggers a labor market signal that indicates reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 triggers a supply-side signal that indicates reduced economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.5 million triggers a supply-side signal that indicates increased economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.5M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 22500 triggers a demand-side signal that indicates reduced economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 22500 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4% and a decline in initial claims below 200000 triggers a labor market signal that indicates reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4% and PAYEMS < 200000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 5% triggers a monetary signal that indicates tightening monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real personal spending above 700000 triggers a demand-side signal that indicates increased economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 700000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
